subject: Physics
options: Both statement I and statement II are correct
Both statement I and statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
solution:
Statement 1- R=80 Ω

When it is cut into 4 equal parts, the resistance of each part will be,

R1=R2=R3=R4=20 Ω

When these resistances are connected in parallel,

Req =204=5 Ω

Hence, the statement I is correct.

Statement 2 -

When the resistances are connected in parallel, the potential difference between them is same. Therefore, the power consumed can be written as,

P=V2R

⇒P1P2=R2R1=23

Hence, statement II is incorrect.

solution:
To measure the emf of a cell we prefer potentiometer rather than voltmeter because
(i) the length of potentiometer which allows greater precision.
(ii) in case of potentiometer, no current flows through the cell.
(iii) of high sensitivity.

solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{eq}}=\mathrm{R}_1+\mathrm{R}_2 \\ & \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{eq}}=150.4+\frac{240 \times 10}{250} \\ & =150.4+9.6=160 \Omega \\ & \mathrm{I}_1=\frac{\mathrm{I} \mathrm{R}_2}{240} \\ & \mathrm{I}_1=\frac{\mathrm{I} \times 9.6}{240} \\ & =\frac{20}{160} \times \frac{9.6}{2400}=\frac{1}{200}=5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~A}=5 \mathrm{~mA}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
In this potentiometer, for zero galvanometer reading, the potential at P must equal the potential at the junction between the 6Ω and 4Ω resistors.
The voltage distribution across the resistors is $V_{junction} = E \times \frac{6}{6+4} = 0.6E$ from point A.
Since the potentiometer wire is uniform with length 50 cm, the potential distribution is also uniform.
Therefore, $\frac{AP}{AB} = \frac{6}{10}$, giving $AP = 50 \times 0.6 = 30$ cm.

subject: Physics
solution:
In a potentiometer, null point or balance point is defined as the point where the deflection of the galvanometer is zero. If emf of the unknown battery is ε then,

ε=(Potential Gradient)×(Balancing length l). Therefore,

ε1ε2=l1l2

⇒1.201.80=36l2

⇒l2=18×3=54 cm

Hence, difference in balancing length is l2-l1=54-36=18 cm

subject: Physics
solution:
When the cells are connected in series is=2ε2r+R=2ε2r+2

When they are connected in parallel, the emf is εp=εr+εr1r+1r=ε and the effective internal resistance will be rp=r×rr+r=r2. Therefore, the current will be,

ip=εR+r2=ε2+r2=2ε4+r

Since, is=ip⇒2ε2r+2=2ε4+r⇒r=2 Ω

subject: Physics
solution:
Colour band A and B indicate the first two significant figures of resistance in 'ohm', while the C band gives the decimal multiplier i.e., the number of zeros that follows the two significant figures A and B.

The last band D indicates the tolerance in percentage about the indicated value or in other word, it represents the percentage accuracy of the indicated value. 

For R1, the given colour of the rings of a carbon resistor are orange, red, brown then, the value of resistance is

R1=32×101

Condition for balance in the Wheat stone's bridge is

R1R3=R2R4

So, 320R3=8040

∴R3=160 Ω, so colour coding of resistor R3 is

Brown→1

Blue→6

Brown→1
16×101⇒160 Ω
